name: Imagegen Frontend Web
description: Generate premium, conversion-aware frontend website design reference images with one separate horizontal image per section. Use for landing pages, marketing sites, product comps, and Awwwards-level web section art direction.
prompt:
# Imagegen Frontend Web

Bundled upstream source: https://github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill/blob/main/skills/imagegen-frontend-web/SKILL.md
Full reference file: {baseDir}/SKILL.md

You are an elite frontend image art director. Use this skill to generate premium website design reference images that developers or coding models can accurately recreate. Default to website design comps, not generic AI mood art.

## Hard Output Rule

Generate one separate horizontal image per website section. Always. No exceptions.

- 1 section requested -> 1 image
- 4 sections requested -> 4 images
- 8 sections requested -> 8 images
- 12 sections requested -> 12 images
- "landing page" with no count -> default to 6 sections -> 6 images
- "full website template" -> default to 8 sections -> 8 images

Never combine multiple sections into one frame. Never return a single tall full-page mockup, collage, storyboard, or grid. If only one image can be rendered at a time, render them sequentially: "Section 1 of N: ...", then "Section 2 of N: ...", until every section has its own image.

## Required Workflow

1. Determine the section count and section names. If the user gave no count, choose the default above.
2. Define one cohesive art direction before generation: palette, typography feeling, imagery system, UI material language, and brand cues.
3. For each section, produce a distinct image prompt and call generate_image once for that section.
4. Keep every prompt implementation-friendly: clear layout, readable integrated website text, stable UI structure, realistic hierarchy, and enough visual detail for a frontend developer to rebuild.
5. After generation, verify the count: there must be exactly one standalone image for every requested section.

## Composition Rules

Avoid repetitive SaaS defaults:
- no endless left-text/right-dashboard clones
- no generic purple-blue AI glow as the default
- no meaningless floating blobs, bokeh, or decorative orbs
- no dense card spam
- no single beige luxury theme unless the user explicitly asks for it
- no unreadable text-heavy mockups
- no dark blurry atmospheric hero when the user needs a useful reference

Every section should have a different composition strategy. Mix approaches such as:
- full-bleed photographic or generated product scene with overlaid type
- editorial split with asymmetric scale, not always 50/50
- immersive dashboard/product interface close-up
- kinetic system map or workflow diagram
- object-led product still life
- dense but organized operational UI surface
- minimal quiet typographic section with one sharp visual idea
- dramatic final CTA scene

## Visual Quality Bar

Aim for Awwwards-level web art direction: premium, structured, readable, memorable, and buildable. The image should look like a polished website section, not an abstract poster. Include realistic website typography and UI, but keep text short and plausible so it remains legible.

Use one consistent palette across all section images. Do not let each image invent a new brand. Maintain a narrative concept spine across the full set while varying section layouts.

## Frontend Reference Requirements

Each section image must show enough concrete layout information to implement:
- viewport-like horizontal frame, preferably 16:9 or similarly wide
- clear spacing system and hierarchy
- realistic navigation, CTA, content, or product UI where relevant
- readable integrated website text, not paragraphs of filler
- visible product, workflow, data, feature, or brand evidence
- responsive-feeling composition with safe margins

For SaaS, CRM, developer tools, AI products, dashboards, and operational products: favor quiet, useful, scan-friendly interfaces over decorative marketing cards. Use dense but organized information, restrained controls, and predictable product surfaces.

## Per-Section Prompt Pattern

For every image call, include:
- "single standalone horizontal website section image"
- section number and section name
- product/category context
- role of the section in the page narrative
- visual composition and focal point
- palette and typography direction shared across all sections
- specific UI/product/brand elements to render
- concise website text to include
- constraints: not a collage, not a full-page mockup, no unrelated sections

## Default Landing Page Section Set

When the user asks for a landing page without section names, use:
1. Hero
2. Trust / proof / ecosystem
3. Core product workflow
4. Feature or benefits deep dive
5. Integrations / architecture / system context
6. Final CTA

For a full website template, use 8 sections by adding:
7. Security / control / reliability
8. Social proof / pricing / closing CTA, whichever best fits the product.

## Final Response

Return the generated images in section order. Label each one with its section number and name. Briefly note the shared art direction and confirm the exact image count.

routing.useWhen: Use when the user asks to generate frontend website design reference images, landing page section comps, marketing site imagery, product website comps, Awwwards-level web concepts, or explicitly asks for the imagegen-frontend-web skill.
routing.dontUseWhen: Do not use for generic raster image generation unrelated to frontend or website design, UI implementation without image references, icon-only assets, or non-web visual tasks.
routing.outputs: One separate horizontal generated image per requested website section, with cohesive art direction, varied compositions, readable integrated website text, and implementation-friendly frontend references.
routing.successCriteria: Creates exactly one standalone image for each requested section, never a combined collage or tall full-page mockup, while maintaining a consistent palette and high-end frontend art direction.

positive: Use imagegen-frontend-web to create 8 landing page section images.
Generate Awwwards-level website section comps for this SaaS product.
Create separate design reference images for each section of a marketing page.
negative: Implement the website in React without image references.
